metadata.description: Opinionated Claude Code plugins for professional coding — language-specific authoring skills and expert subagents.

plugins.name: rust-dev
plugins.description: Idiomatic Rust authoring: progressive-disclosure skill plus rust-expert authoring subagent and slash commands for review and refactor.

plugins.name: android-dev
plugins.description: Android development plugin: Gradle build management, UI/UX design with Material 3 and Figma including a screenshot-grading evaluator loop (emulator captures scored against a weighted design rubric by a separate evaluator), multi-device emulator orchestration via bundled MCP stack, mock server generation, automated screenshot capture across phone and tablet form factors, release signing, and end-to-end publishing flows for F-Droid and Google Play.

plugins.name: plugin-dev
plugins.description: Lean, security-aware authoring kit for Claude Code plugins and the shared cross-host core of the platform-authoring family (cowork-dev, cursor-dev, codex-dev, opencode-dev, hermes-dev, openclaw-dev), with a balanced deterministic/LLM split: a deterministic bash validation suite (validate-plugin.sh orchestrating per-domain validators on a shared JSON finding contract) plus scaffolders own mechanical structure and checks, while the LLM lane owns judgment (leak/injection review, triggering, design). The boundary is portable: install-kit.sh + scaffold-validator.sh vendor a self-contained deterministic lane into ANY plugin, the determinism-boundary skill teaches the split, and create-plugin / refactor-plugin make new and existing plugins balanced like plugin-dev itself. Skills for plugin structure / skill / command / agent / hook / MCP authoring, cross-host subagent scaffolding (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode), leak-audit and best-practices-sync passes, plus skill-workshop which mines session JSONL history (via the session-analyzer Haiku subagent) for skill candidates; agents for validation, skill review, agent generation, and session mining. Current to June 2026: 32 hook events, five handler types, userConfig/channels/dependencies manifest fields, monitors/bin/LSP surfaces.

plugins.name: cowork-dev
plugins.description: Authoring kit for Claude Cowork plugins, current as of June 2026. Owns the Cowork-specific half of plugin development: the four install paths (Anthropic catalog, direct ZIP upload, marketplace by URL, org private marketplaces) with their real limits (200 MB / 5,000 files per plugin, 50 MB manual ZIP, npm/pip marketplace sources unsupported, reserved-name list), component support (skills surface in Cowork + Claude chat; hooks and sub-agents run only in Cowork; connectors via Anthropic's cloud — local stdio MCP is NOT supported), org distribution states (Required / Installed by default / Available / Not available), and chat-native design patterns (multilingual skill triggers, connector-aware enrichment, privacy posture for cloud Routines, refuse-without-prerequisites, save-and-resume). Ships a deterministic lane: validate-cowork-artifact.sh checks a Cowork-bound plugin package for name/reserved-name violations, package-size and file-count limits, npm/pip marketplace sources, and stdio MCP servers, on the shared JSON finding contract.

plugins.name: cursor-dev
plugins.description: Authoring kit for Cursor (the AI editor) extensions, current as of June 2026, Cursor 3.7. Owns the Cursor-specific half of extension development: plugins (.cursor-plugin/plugin.json manifest, marketplace.json multi-plugin repos, cursor.com/marketplace publishing with its open-source requirement, Team Marketplaces from private GitHub repos), rules (.cursor/rules/*.mdc only — plain .md is silently ignored — with the four application types and Team > Project > User precedence), skills (agentskills.io standard with Cursor's extra discovery paths including legacy .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/, the paths glob-visibility key, disable-model-invocation, and the commands-to-skills migration), hooks (~22 camelCase events in hooks.json with version field, prompt-type LLM hooks, permission allow/deny/ask protocol — deliberately NOT Claude Code's schema), subagents (.cursor/agents/ plus .claude/.codex compat reads, no tools field), and MCP (mcp.json transports, OAuth, ${env:NAME} interpolation, install deeplinks, permissions.json). Ships a deterministic lane: validate-cursor-artifact.sh checks a Cursor-bound artifact directory for manifest validity, .mdc frontmatter parse and rule-type combos, ignored plain-.md rules, skill name/directory mismatches, and unknown hook event names, on the shared JSON finding contract.

plugins.name: codex-dev
plugins.description: Authoring kit for OpenAI Codex (CLI + IDE extension) extensions, current as of June 2026, Codex CLI v0.139. Owns the Codex-specific half of extension development: plugins (.codex-plugin/plugin.json manifest, skills + hooks + MCP + app bundling, repo and personal marketplaces under .agents/plugins/, codex marketplace add, curated first-party plugins), skills (agentskills.io SKILL.md plus the Codex-only agents/openai.yaml sidecar, the five-tier discovery order, the ~2%-of-context catalog cap, custom-prompt deprecation and migration), subagents (one-TOML-per-agent custom agents with required developer_instructions, [agents] runtime limits, explicit-only spawning), config.toml (user + project layering, the v0.134.0 BREAKING removal of [profiles.*] and top-level profile= in favor of standalone overlay files, model/provider keys, approval and sandbox policies, [features], [tools], MCP server tables), the hooks engine (10 lifecycle events, hooks.json/TOML locations and precedence, exit-code and JSON output contracts, the /hooks trust model for non-managed command hooks), and AGENTS.md discovery (override files, git-root-to-cwd walk, project_doc_max_bytes). Ships a deterministic lane: validate-codex-artifact.sh checks .codex-plugin manifests and component pointers, SKILL.md frontmatter, agent TOML required fields, legacy [profiles.*]/profile= residue in config.toml (codex-legacy-profile), and hooks.json event names, on the shared JSON finding contract.

plugins.name: release-promo
plugins.description: Drafts release-announcement posts for the platforms a project actually belongs on. Skills for Reddit (subreddit-aware, including Matrix subs r/matrixprotocol and r/matrixdotorg, plus r/selfhosted, r/programming, language and topic subs), This Week in Matrix, Hacker News Show HN, Lobsters, and Fediverse. The /promote-release command surveys the repo, picks eligible channels, and drafts one post per channel. Never autoposts.

plugins.name: stingy-agents
plugins.description: Three scope-bounded subagents that let a skill or an Opus caller offload bulk or mechanical work to a cheaper, faster model: readonly-scanner (Haiku) for bulk file I/O and grep, skill-rewriter (Sonnet) for spec-driven edits to existing files, and code-generator (Sonnet) for spec-driven file creation. Spend Opus tokens on judgment calls, Haiku and Sonnet tokens on everything else.

plugins.name: infra-build
plugins.description: Make a project buildable by the three publishing pipelines under ~/dev/infra: utils (Debian .deb via pkgskel + reprepro), build-for-mac (Rust → macOS .pkg via GitHub Actions), and publish-images (multi-arch Docker → DockerHub/GHCR/Quay/GitLab/ECR). Skills audit readiness, scaffold the missing file layout, and register the project in both the data file and the workflow YAML in lockstep so the two never drift.

plugins.name: git-github
plugins.description: Everyday git/GitHub operations for Claude Code: draft commits and PRs that match the repo's style, run a single-tool authoritative code review, dispatch the same diff to sibling CLIs (codex/gemini/opencode) for multi-model second opinions, audit code comments for explanatory value, audit GitHub Actions workflows for errors and outdated actions, review and tighten READMEs, audit dependency licenses and pick a compatible project LICENSE, and cut release tags with optional GitHub releases. Skills auto-trigger on natural language; nothing autoposts, force-pushes, or rewrites history. Hands off announcement drafting to release-promo.

plugins.name: planning
plugins.description: Nine-skill pipeline that turns a vague idea into executed work, keeps each project's contracts honest, and gives a cross-project portfolio view. brainstorming validates the design via question-driven exploration; planning-projects produces a staged plan with phase gates and Red-Green cycles (reconciling backlog and workflow specs at research time, with live-artifact gate checks on user-facing stages); executing-plans drives the plan with stage-gate checks, a default independent-evaluator pass at gates and close-out, stage-boundary handoff notes for context resets, opens/closes backlog entries, and audits workflow specs at close-out; dispatching-parallel-agents fans out independent tasks marked Parallel YES; no-fafo-debugging enforces evidence-first root-cause analysis when plans hit failures; backlog owns the deferred-work register at docs/backlog.md (now with `unify` and `complete` subcommands); workflow-spec owns the behavior contracts at docs/workflows/ used to detect regressions after change; project-maturity scaffolds and audits a per-project docs/MATURITY.md across six publishing-readiness axes (Documentation, Security, Packaging, UI/UX, i18n, Testing & CI) — v0.5.3 treats Google Play, F-Droid, IzzyOnDroid, GitHub Releases (signed APK, auto-detected), Obtainium, and Accrescent as peer Android distribution channels so projects that deliberately skip Play remain ship-ready; portfolio is a cross-project orchestrator that scans ~/dev/, unifies plans+backlogs per project, rolls up a global backlog index and a global maturity dashboard at ~/.claude/. Skills hand off to each other and were designed as a unit.

plugins.name: browser-extensions
plugins.description: Authoring and shipping browser extensions (WebExtensions) for Chrome, Firefox, and Firefox for Android. Skill `browser-extensions` covers manifest v3 migration, content scripts, permissions, store rejections, and the Firefox-for-Android caveats. Skill `amo-compliance-check` runs a preflight audit (manifest, permissions justification, minified-source rules, addon ID) before submission to addons.mozilla.org and ships a `scripts/amo-check.py` linter.

plugins.name: loadout
plugins.description: Per-project + per-task plugin scoping. Layers a sticky tech baseline (android/rust/web-ext/python/plugin-dev/...) with on-demand task overlays (security-audit/release/wiki/refactor/...) and writes the resulting enabledPlugins map to .claude/settings.local.json — so only the plugins relevant to the current work load at session start. Detects the tech baseline automatically on first entry to a project via SessionStart hook; toggle overlays with /loadout +<task> and /loadout -<task>.

plugins.name: i18n
plugins.description: Internationalization for any project that has user-facing strings. Skill `i18n-audit` detects the project's i18n framework (gettext .po, i18next/react-intl/Vue i18n/next-intl JSON, Android strings.xml, iOS .strings/.stringsdict/.xcstrings, Flutter ARB, Rails YAML, Django gettext, Qt .ts, .NET .resx, Java .properties) via Python signature scripts and runs detect-framework.py, scan-hardcoded.py (heuristic finder for likely user-facing strings still in source — TSV with file/line/likelihood for LLM triage), and diff-catalogs.py (per-locale missing/extra/placeholder-mismatch report). Skill `i18n-translate` orchestrates an LLM translation pass via the sonnet-pinned `translator` subagent that preserves placeholders (printf, ICU MessageFormat, Handlebars, positional Android format specifiers) and expands plurals to the target's CLDR categories (en/de/es: one+other; ru/pl/uk: one+few+many+other; ar: zero+one+two+few+many+other; ja/ko/zh: other only); ships extract-missing.py and validate-placeholders.py with brace-balanced ICU parsing, printf-type-signature check, HTML tag balance, and per-locale CLDR plural-category presence check. Skill `i18n-formats` documents per-format escaping, plural-form headers, and metadata gotchas. Commands: /i18n-audit, /i18n-fill-gaps (parallel per-locale dispatch), /i18n-add-locale (scaffold + first-pass translate). Nothing autocommits.

plugins.name: game-dev
plugins.description: Designs and reviews game mechanics, game feel, camera/navigation, UX/FTUE, accessibility, and code architecture for Godot 4, Unity 6, and Unreal 5. Every rule cites the source it came from — Schell, Sylvester, Swink, Nystrom, Hodent, Nesky's 50 Camera Mistakes, and the Game Accessibility Guidelines. Use /game-review for a scoped diff review (mechanics + feel + camera + UX + a11y + architecture) that plays a runnable build before grading feel/UX and labels findings reviewed-live vs static-only, and /game-mechanic for a guided mechanic-design session that outputs an implementable brief. Sonnet-pinned game-design-expert subagent handles all dispatches; nothing is auto-applied.
